bugs.title: Three-dot menu popup clipped — cannot escape slide container overflow
bugs.description: On the My Lists screen (mobile), tapping the three-dot menu on a list item opens a dropdown that is clipped and not fully visible. The dropdown is cut off at the boundary of the slide container rather than rendering on top of surrounding content.
bugs.rootCause: The `.slideContainer` in App.module.css (lines 148-152) has `overflow: hidden`, which is needed for the iOS push/pop slide animations but also clips the absolutely-positioned `.menuDropdown`. A prior fix removed `overflow: hidden` from `.lists` in ListSelector.module.css, but the dropdown still can't escape `.slideContainer`. The DOM chain is: `.slideContainer` (overflow:hidden) → `.listScreen` → `.mobileFullScreen` → ListSelector `.lists` → `.listItem` → `.menuWrap` (position:relative) → `.menuDropdown` (position:absolute).
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport)
Log in and go to the Lists tab
Ensure at least one list exists
Tap the three-dot menu button on any list item
Observe: the dropdown menu is cut off / not fully visible
bugs.suggestedFix:
The `overflow: hidden` on `.slideContainer` is needed for slide animations but kills the dropdown. Best approaches:

**Option A (recommended for iOS feel): Render the menu as a mobile-style bottom sheet / action sheet.** On mobile, instead of a small positioned dropdown, render the menu options as a full-width fixed-position bottom sheet overlay. This sidesteps the overflow issue entirely and is the most native iOS pattern.

**Option B: React portal.** In ListSelector.jsx, render `.menuDropdown` via `ReactDOM.createPortal(dropdown, document.body)` so it escapes all overflow contexts. Position it using the menu button's `getBoundingClientRect()`.

**Option C: Toggle overflow on `.slideContainer` when menu is open.** Temporarily set `overflow: visible` when a menu is open. Risky if a slide transition fires while menu is open.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-dot menu options are fully visible when opened on any list item (first, middle, last)
Menu renders above all other content (proper z-index / stacking)
Slide animations (push/pop) still work correctly — no content leaking outside the container
No visual regression on the iOS grouped table view styling (rounded corners preserved)
Works on both mobile and desktop viewports
Works in both light and dark mode
bugs.notes: A prior fix removed overflow:hidden from .lists — that was correct but insufficient. The real blocker is .slideContainer in App.module.css which also has overflow:hidden (needed for slide animations). The fix must work around it, not remove it.

bugs.title: Simplify mobile header — remove all controls and logo text
bugs.description: On mobile (≤700px), the header should be a slim, empty bar that serves as a container for contextual actions only (e.g., '+' for new list, share button on detail view). Currently it still shows the app logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out button. Username/ThemeToggle/Sign Out now live in the Settings tab. The logo text is unnecessary on mobile and wastes vertical space. Desktop header should remain unchanged (logo + username + ThemeToggle + Sign Out).
bugs.rootCause: The header JSX in App.jsx (lines 265-276) renders the logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out unconditionally with no mobile-specific conditional rendering. The `isMobile` hook is already imported but not used in the header.
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport) or resize browser
Observe the header — logo, username, dark mode toggle, and Sign Out button are all visible
These controls are redundant with the Settings tab, and the logo wastes vertical space
bugs.suggestedFix:
In App.jsx, conditionally render header contents based on `isMobile`: ```jsx
{!isMobile && ( <>

ShoppingListAI

{isGuest ? 'Guest' : user.displayName ?? user.email}
)}
``` On mobile the header renders as a slim empty bar. Contextual actions ('+' new list, share) can be added to this header in a follow-up or within the individual tab/detail views as they are now. Alternatively, hide the entire header on mobile via CSS if no contextual actions are needed in it yet: ```css @media (max-width: 700px) { .header { display: none; } } ``` But keeping the header element (empty) is preferred so contextual actions can be slotted in later.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (≤700px), the header does not show the logo text, username, ThemeToggle, or Sign Out
On mobile, the header remains as a slim bar (not removed entirely) for future contextual actions
On desktop (≥701px), the header continues to show logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out (no change)
The Settings tab on mobile still works with dark mode toggle and sign out
No extra vertical space wasted on mobile — header should be minimal height when empty
Works in both light and dark mode
bugs.notes: Combines two pieces of feedback: (1) username/ThemeToggle/Sign Out are redundant with Settings tab, (2) logo text wastes vertical space on mobile. The header bar itself should remain for future contextual actions but render empty/slim on mobile.

bugs.title: Mobile header still takes up vertical space — hide it completely
bugs.description: On mobile (≤700px), the header element still renders as an empty bar occupying vertical space even though its contents were removed in BUG-002. The header should be completely hidden on mobile via `display: none` so it doesn't waste any screen real estate. Desktop header remains unchanged.
bugs.rootCause: BUG-002 removed the header contents on mobile using conditional JSX (`{!isMobile && (...)}`), but the `<header>` element itself still renders. The ≤700px media query at line 246 sets `min-height: 0` and `padding: env(safe-area-inset-top, 0)` — but the ≤480px media query at line 273 overrides with `padding: 1rem 0 0.75rem`, adding visible vertical space for the empty header. Even without the 480px override, the empty element still takes up some space.
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport)
Observe: there is an empty gap at the top of the screen where the header bar is rendered but contains nothing
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (≤700px), the header element is not visible and takes up zero vertical space
Safe-area-inset-top spacing is preserved for PWA/notched devices (applied to .app or another container if needed)
On desktop (≥701px), the header is fully visible with logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out (no change)
Works in both light and dark mode
bugs.notes: Follow-up to BUG-002 which removed header contents but left the empty element visible.
